completeness: Negative paths covered (e.g. 'Members cannot exceed five active loans', 'Suspended members cannot borrow', 'Duplicate ISBNs are rejected', 'Returned loans cannot be returned again', 'Stale notifications expire'); no Scenario Outline / boundary cases beyond AC list (e.g. no parametrized 4-vs-5-vs-6 loan boundary, no fine threshold sweep)
alignment: Every scenario carries an @AC tag (e.g. '@AC-3.2\n  Scenario: Members cannot exceed five active loans')
Scenario names are human-readable AC restatements: 'Returning a reserved book notifies the head of the queue', 'A notified reservation can be fulfilled by checkout'
Background blocks remove setup duplication (us-3-lending.feature: shared books/members table); one AC per scenario; no Scenario Outline / per-row Examples tagging is used
declarative: Good: 'When the librarian lends "Dune" to Alice', 'Then Alice has 1 active loan', 'Then Bob's reservation for "Dune" is "notified"'

Bad — HTTP status codes leak into Then steps repeatedly: 'Then the response status is 201', 'Then the response status is 409', 'Then the response status is 404', 'Then the response status is 402', 'Then the response status is 403' — a librarian would not recognise these. Also magic numbers 'unpaid fines of 501 cents', 'is 3 days overdue' rather than named domain constants like <fine threshold> or <loan period>.
atomic: Atomic: 'When the librarian lends "Dune" to Alice'

Atomic chain via And, not compound: 'Given Alice has borrowed "Dune"
    And Bob reserves "Dune"
    And the librarian returns Alice's loan for "Dune"
    And Bob's notification for "Dune" is stale' — separate steps each, no compound 'and ... and' inside one step
All 198 steps in bdd-report.json show status 'passed' (0 failed/undefined), so step definitions are wired up and do not cap completeness
